text_blocks.text: Luther wrote "Ach Gott, vom Ilimmel sieh darein"
("Oh God, look down from heaven"). "Num komm, der Heiden
Meiland" (Now come, Savior of the gentiles), based on
Veni redemption gentium, became the main hymn (Hauptlied)
for Advent. He transformed A solus ortus carbine to
"Christum vir sollen loben schon" ("We should now praise
Christ") and Veni Creator Spiritus to "Komm, Gott
Schopfer, Heiliger Geist" ("Come, Holy Spirit, Lord God").
He wrote two hymns on the Ten Commandments, "Dies sind
die heilgen Zehn Gebot" and "Mensch, willst du leben
seliglich". His "Solobet seist du, Jesu Christ" ("Pruise be to
You, Jesus Christ") became the main hymn for Christmas.
He wrote for Pentecost "Nun bitten wir den Heiligen Geist",
and adopted for Easter "Christ ist erstanden" (Christ is risen),
based on Victimae paschali laudes. "Mit Tried und Freud
ich fahr dahin", a paraphrase of Nunc dimittis, was intended
for Purification, but became also a funeral hymn. He
paraphrased the Te Deum as "Herr Gott, dich loben wir"
with a simplified form of the melody. It became known as
the German Te Deum.
